semanticCore: Sponsorship, portion, folder, double, or guarantee
quranicRelevance: Sponsoring/caring for someone (Kaffala - 'wa kaffalaha zakariyya'), and portion/share (Kifl - 'yu'tikum kiflayni min rahmatihi').

keyDerivations.meaning: To make sponsor, sponsor, guard, or care for

figurativeOrRareUsages: يُؤْتِكُمْ كِفْلَيْنِ مِنْ رَحْمَتِهِ (57:28): He will give you double portions/shares of His mercy.
ذَا الْكِفْلِ: Dhul-Kifl (prophetic name, classically meaning 'possessor of double portion/responsibility').
discardedNoise: Foster parenting regulations in modern orphanages.
Obscure details of camel saddle pads (كِفل).
summaryEn: The root denotes custody and portions, covering Zachariah's sponsorship (Kafala) of Mary, double shares of mercy (Kifl), and legal guarantees.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الكفالة والتحمل، مغطياً كفالة زكريا لمريم عليها السلام، والنصيب المضاعف من الأجر والرحمة (كِفْلين).
rawText: كَفَالَةٌ Responsibility; answerableness; amenability; or suretiship; (S, Mgh, Msb, K;) the conjoining of one responsibility (ذِمَّة) to another, [i. e., the conjoining one's own responsibility to that of another person,] with respect to the right of suit, [so that one person becomes liable to be sued for that which another owes]; (Mgh;) i. q. ضَمَانٌ. (S, &c.) كَفَلٌ The عَجُز [or hinder part, posteriors, buttocks, or rump]: (Msb, K:) or the رِدْف [or hindermost part] thereof: or the [part called] قَطَن. (K.) كَفَلَ بَالمَالِ 1 He was, or became, responsible, answerable, accountable, amenable, surety, or guarantee, for the property (Msb) owed by another person: (IbrD:) [it may be rendered he guaranteed the property. See مَكْفُولٌ]. === كَفَلَ بَالنَّفْسِ He was, or became, responsible, answerable, amenable, or surety, for another person, (Msb,) i. e., for the latter's appearance, or presence, to answer a suit. (IbrD.) كَفِيلٌ One who is responsible, answerable, amenable, or a sponsor or surety. (S, K, &c.) مَكْفُولٌ app. signifies Guaranteed, or pledged: for, accord. to IKtt, as is said in the Msb, you say كَفَلْتُ المَالَ as well as كَفَلْتُ بِالمَالِ; meaning I took upon myself the property; became responsible, or answerable, for it; [or I guaranteed it:] or مَكْفُولٌ is better rendered ensured by an acknowledgment of responsibility for it: see an ex. voce مَرْهُونٌ. مِكْفَالٌ A woman large in the كَفَل [or hinder part, or posteriors]. (TA in art. ثقل.)
